name: Portillo del Infierno Trail
name_original: Portillo del Infierno — circular desde Villadiego
description: This moderate 30.1-mile mountain biking loop from Villadiego takes you through diverse terrain and the dramatic Portillo del Infierno gorge.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villadiego offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, valleys, and varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. The region, located in the province of Burgos, Spain, features a mix of open country and natural features that provide engaging mountain bike trails. Riders can expect routes that traverse through natural areas, offering both scenic views and physical challenges. Best mountain bike trails around Villadiego The most popular mountain bike trail is San Antón Valley and Úrbel del Castillo Loop, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 17 minutes to complete, winding through valleys and past historical sites. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Portillo del Infierno Trail, a moderate 30.1 miles (48.5 km) path. This route features varied terrain and notable elevation gain, providing a challenging experience. Local mountain bikers also love the R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route, a 17.2 miles (27.6 km) trail leading through the Riomance Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Villadiego is defined by its valleys, rolling hills, and diverse natural features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to more challenging routes. The routes in Villadiego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 11 reviews. More than 52 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villadiego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villadiego?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Villadiego, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 50 times, highlighting the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villadiego?
A: Mountain biking around Villadiego features a diverse landscape with rolling hills, valleys, and varied terrain. You'll find a mix of open country and natural features, providing both scenic views and physical challenges.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Villadiego?
A: Yes, Villadiego offers several easy mountain bike trails. An excellent option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Villaute loop from Las Hormazas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.3 miles (15 km) long and has a moderate elevation gain, making it a good introduction to the area's trails.

Q: What are some of the longer, more challenging mountain bike routes in the Villadiego area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164953202/las-loras-r14-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Loras R14 Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great option. It spans 32.8 miles (52.7 km) with significant elevation changes, offering a demanding experience through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Villadiego?
A: Yes, many routes in Villadiego are designed as loops. A popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100342319/san-anton-valley-and-urbel-del-castillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Antón Valley and Úrbel del Castillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 29.5 miles (47.5 km) and takes you through valleys and past historical sites.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Villadiego?
A: The trails around Villadiego offer access to several interesting natural features and historical sites. You might encounter the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona&lt;/a&gt; natural monument, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4381459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CastroAmaya&lt;/a&gt; historical site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164931802/r3-riomance-valley-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;R3 - Riomance Valley MTB Route&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads directly through the scenic Riomance Valley.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Villadiego?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villadiego are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 11 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic valleys, and the engaging challenges offered by routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103325912/portillo-del-infierno-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo del Infierno Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging workout?
A: Yes, many trails in Villadiego feature notable elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103325912/portillo-del-infierno-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo del Infierno Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes over 600 meters of ascent, providing a robust workout for experienced riders.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villadiego?
A: While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking in Villadiego. The temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant, offering comfortable riding conditions.

Q: How do the mountain bike trails in Villadiego compare to those in the wider Burgos province?
A: Villadiego, located within the province of Burgos, offers a representative sample of the region's mountain biking. It is characterized by rolling hills and valleys, similar to much of Burgos, but with a unique blend of open country and historical sites that provide distinct riding experiences.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options in Villadiego?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, the easier trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Villaute loop from Las Hormazas&lt;/a&gt;, with its shorter distance and lower elevation gain, could be suitable for families with some biking experience.
tips: The Villadiego bridge is an example of civil architecture that is made up of three arches, the central one being higher and wider than the side ones. It is made of stone, its construction is ashlar and it has a vaulted structure.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Portillo del Infierno mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. It demands good fitness and advanced riding skills due to its varied terrain and significant elevation gain. Some sections may even require you to push your bike, placing it within the STS S2 difficulty range.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll start with progressive inclines on dirt tracks and some singletrack. The middle section becomes more challenging with significant ascents and technical descents over stony paths and stone slabs. The return leg offers gentler slopes, transitioning back to dirt tracks and scenic riverside trails.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Portillo del Infierno circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is a loop starting and ending in Villadiego, so you can typically find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas in Villadiego to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Portillo del Infierno route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to tackle this route. The weather is usually milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring challenging conditions like mud or snow, especially in higher sections.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking within the Las Loras UNESCO Global Geopark?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located within the Las Loras UNESCO Global Geopark, it's important to respect local regulations. Generally, this means staying on marked trails, not disturbing wildlife or geological formations, and practicing Leave No Trace principles. There are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Geopark, but always check for any local updates before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Portillo del Infierno trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are often allowed on trails in Spain, given the technical nature and length of this mountain bike route, it might be challenging for some dogs. If you bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, accustomed to long, strenuous rides, and always keep them on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock. Remember to carry enough water for both you and your pet.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting features. The route passes by the Medieval bridge over the Brullés River (Villadiego), offers views from the Path Below Peña Ulaña, and includes the impressive Peña Castillo with its sculpted rock faces. The dramatic "Portillo del Infierno" gorge itself is a significant highlight.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride parts of the R14 - Las Loras, Sendero de la Gargantilla, R16 - Ribera del Brullés, and Cañada del Monte, among others, offering a diverse riding experience.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink in the villages along the route?
faqs.answer: The circular route passes through several small villages such as Tablada de Villadiego, Villalbilla de Villadiego, Hormicedo, Ordejón de Arriba, and Arenillas de Villadiego. While Villadiego itself offers cafes and pubs, options in the smaller villages might be limited, especially outside of peak season. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions. A helmet is essential, and consider knee/elbow pads for the technical sections. A GPS device or a downloaded map is highly recommended as some sections can be remote.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is not recommended for families with young children or beginner mountain bikers. It's classified as moderate and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills, with technical sections and significant elevation gain. Beginners might find it overly challenging and potentially unsafe.

waypoints.name: Medieval bridge over the Brullés River (Villadiego)
